Title: Create user with valid data
Preconditions: 1. Server is running at http://localhost:5000 | 2. Database is initialized with users table | 3. Frontend is running at http://localhost:3000 | 4. Navigate to /users page
Steps: 1. Fill Name field with valid name | 2. Fill Date of Birth with valid date (18+ years old) | 3. Fill Address field with valid address (min 5 chars) | 4. Click "Add User" button | 5. Verify success message appears | 6. Check user appears in table below
Test Data: Name: "John Doe" | DOB: "2000-01-15" | Address: "123 Main Street, City, State, 12345"
Expected Result: 1. Success alert message displays "User added successfully!" | 2. Form fields are cleared | 3. New user appears in the users table with correct data | 4. User count increases by 1 | 5. No errors in console

Title: Complete user lifecycle from creation to display
Preconditions: 1. Server and frontend running | 2. Clean database state | 3. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Navigate to /users from home page | 2. Verify empty state message if no users | 3. Fill all form fields with valid data | 4. Submit form | 5. Verify API POST request succeeds | 6. Verify database insert | 7. Verify GET request fetches updated list | 8. Verify new user displays in table | 9. Navigate away and back to /users | 10. Verify user persists in table
Test Data: Name: "Jane Smith" | DOB: "1995-05-20" | Address: "456 Oak Avenue, Town, Province, 67890"
Expected Result: 1. Navigation from home works | 2. Form submission triggers POST /api/users | 3. Response contains user ID and created_at | 4. Users list auto-refreshes | 5. Table displays Name, DOB, Address, Created At columns | 6. Data persists after navigation | 7. All API calls return success | 8. Database has new record

Title: Submit form with name containing special characters
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Enter name with hyphen: "Mary-Jane Watson" | 2. Enter valid DOB | 3. Enter valid address | 4. Submit form | 5. Repeat with apostrophe: "O'Brien" | 6. Repeat with multiple words: "Jean-Pierre D'Angelo"
Test Data: Names: "Mary-Jane", "O'Brien", "Jean-Pierre D'Angelo" | DOB: "1990-03-10" | Address: "789 Elm Street"
Expected Result: 1. Form accepts hyphens in names | 2. Form accepts apostrophes in names | 3. Form accepts multiple spaces and special chars | 4. Validation passes | 5. User created successfully | 6. Special characters display correctly in table

Title: Test with minimum valid DOB (exactly 18 years old)
Steps: 1. Calculate date exactly 18 years ago from today | 2. Enter name | 3. Enter calculated DOB | 4. Enter address | 5. Submit form
Test Data: Name: "Young User" | DOB: [today minus 18 years] | Address: "111 Pine Road"
Expected Result: 1. Form accepts DOB of exactly 18 years | 2. No age validation error | 3. User created successfully | 4. DOB displays correctly in table with proper formatting

Title: Test with very long address (max length boundary)
Steps: 1. Enter valid name | 2. Enter valid DOB | 3. Enter address with 500 characters (near database TEXT limit) | 4. Submit form | 5. Verify address displays in table
Test Data: Name: "Address Test" | DOB: "1985-07-12" | Address: [500-character string with full address details]
Expected Result: 1. Form accepts long address | 2. No truncation errors | 3. User created successfully | 4. Address displays in table (may be truncated in UI with ellipsis) | 5. Tooltip or expansion shows full address

Title: Empty users list state
Preconditions: 1. Fresh database with no users | 2. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Navigate to /users | 2. Observe the users table section
Expected Result: 1. Empty state message displays: "No users found. Add your first user above!" | 2. Table headers are not shown | 3. No loading spinner visible | 4. Form is still accessible and functional

Title: Navigation to /users page from home
Preconditions: 1. User is on home page (/) | 2. Server and frontend running
Steps: 1. Click on "Users" link in navigation bar | 2. Verify URL changes to /users | 3. Verify page loads correctly
Expected Result: 1. URL is /users | 2. Page title shows "User Management" | 3. Navigation link highlights/shows active state | 4. Form and table sections are visible | 5. Page styling matches Oktawave branding

Title: Navigation to /users page from CustomerForm
Steps: 1. Click "Users" button in top-right navigation | 2. Verify URL changes to /users
Expected Result: 1. URL is /users | 2. Page loads without errors | 3. No navigation conflicts | 4. Active page indicator updates | 5. All navigation links (Home, Apply, Users) are visible

Title: Navigation to /users page from KYC page
Steps: 1. Click "Users" button in navigation | 2. Verify URL changes to /users
Expected Result: 1. URL is /users | 2. Page loads successfully | 3. No data loss from previous page | 4. Navigation works bidirectionally

Title: Display multiple users in table
Preconditions: 1. Database has 5+ users | 2. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Verify table loads | 2. Check all columns are visible | 3. Verify data formatting | 4. Check table scrolling if needed
Expected Result: 1. All users display in table | 2. Columns: Name, Date of Birth, Address, Created At | 3. Dates formatted as "MMM DD, YYYY" | 4. Names highlighted in Oktawave primary color (#2DB5DA) | 5. Addresses show in gray (#939598) | 6. Table is scrollable horizontally on small screens

Title: Submit form with empty name field
Steps: 1. Leave Name field empty | 2. Fill DOB with valid date | 3. Fill Address with valid text | 4. Click "Add User" button
Expected Result: 1. Form validation error appears: "Name is required" | 2. Field border turns red | 3. Error message displays below Name field | 4. Form does not submit | 5. No API call is made | 6. Users table does not update

Title: Submit form with name less than 2 characters
Steps: 1. Enter single character in Name field | 2. Fill other fields with valid data | 3. Submit form
Expected Result: 1. Validation error: "Name must be at least 2 characters" | 2. Name field shows error state | 3. Submit blocked | 4. No API call made

Title: Submit form with name containing numbers
Steps: 1. Enter "John123" in Name field | 2. Fill other fields validly | 3. Submit form
Expected Result: 1. Validation error: "Name can only contain letters, spaces, hyphens, and apostrophes" | 2. Field highlights error | 3. Form submission prevented

Title: Submit form with empty DOB field
Steps: 1. Fill Name with valid text | 2. Leave Date of Birth empty | 3. Fill Address | 4. Submit form
Expected Result: 1. Error displays: "Date of birth is required" | 2. DOB field shows error state | 3. Form does not submit | 4. Error clears when user enters date

Title: Submit form with future DOB
Steps: 1. Enter valid name | 2. Select tomorrow's date for DOB | 3. Enter valid address | 4. Submit
Test Data: Name: "Future User" | DOB: [tomorrow's date] | Address: "111 Blvd"
Expected Result: 1. Validation error: "Date of birth must be in the past" | 2. DOB field highlighted in red | 3. Submit blocked

Title: Submit form with DOB for under 18 user
Steps: 1. Enter valid name | 2. Enter DOB of 10 years ago | 3. Enter valid address | 4. Submit
Test Data: Name: "Young User" | DOB: [10 years ago] | Address: "222 Lane"
Expected Result: 1. Error: "You must be at least 18 years old" | 2. Field shows error | 3. Form blocked

Title: Submit form with empty address field
Steps: 1. Fill Name | 2. Fill DOB | 3. Leave Address empty | 4. Submit
Expected Result: 1. Error: "Address is required" | 2. Address field error state | 3. Submit prevented

Title: Submit form with address less than 5 characters
Steps: 1. Fill Name validly | 2. Fill DOB validly | 3. Enter "123" in Address | 4. Submit
Expected Result: 1. Error: "Please enter a valid address" | 2. Address field error | 3. No submission

Title: Submit all empty fields
Steps: 1. Click "Add User" without filling any fields
Expected Result: 1. All three fields show error messages | 2. Multiple validation errors displayed | 3. Error alert at top: "Please fix the errors before submitting." | 4. Form blocked | 5. No API call

Title: Test server error handling during user creation
Preconditions: 1. Backend server stopped or database unavailable | 2. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Fill form with valid data | 2. Submit | 3. Observe error handling
Expected Result: 1. Loading state shows briefly | 2. Error alert displays: "An error occurred while adding the user. Please try again." | 3. Form fields retain entered data | 4. User can retry submission | 5. Console shows network error

Title: Test server error handling during user list fetch
Preconditions: 1. Backend server stopped | 2. User navigates to /users
Steps: 1. Navigate to /users page | 2. Observe behavior
Expected Result: 1. Loading spinner may show briefly | 2. Error message displays: "Failed to load users. Please try again." | 3. Empty state or error state shown | 4. Form is still functional | 5. Retry mechanism available

Title: Verify brand compliance - Colors
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Inspect UI elements
Steps: 1. Check navigation link active state color | 2. Check primary button color | 3. Check table header color | 4. Check user name color in table | 5. Check address text color | 6. Inspect hover states
Expected Result: 1. Primary color #2DB5DA used for: active nav, headings gradient, submit button, table headers, user names | 2. Secondary color #939598 used for: inactive nav links, address text, helper text | 3. Gradient backgrounds use specified colors | 4. Hover states apply color with transparency

Title: Verify brand compliance - Typography
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Inspect fonts in DevTools
Steps: 1. Check body font-family | 2. Check headings font | 3. Verify fallback fonts | 4. Check font weights
Expected Result: 1. Primary font: Proxima Nova | 2. Fallback font: Montserrat | 3. System fallbacks present | 4. Headings use correct weight (600-700) | 5. Body text readable | 6. Consistent font sizing

Title: Test form behavior during submission (button disabled state)
Steps: 1. Fill form with valid data | 2. Click "Add User" | 3. Immediately try clicking again | 4. Observe button state during submission
Expected Result: 1. Button shows "Adding..." text during submission | 2. Button is disabled while submitting | 3. Cannot submit duplicate request | 4. Button re-enables after success or error | 5. Loading state provides feedback

Title: Verify date formatting in users table
Preconditions: 1. Database has users with various creation dates | 2. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Check "Created At" column | 2. Verify date format | 3. Check "Date of Birth" column format
Expected Result: 1. Dates formatted as "MMM DD, YYYY" (e.g., "Jan 15, 2023") | 2. Format consistent across all rows | 3. Dates are human-readable | 4. No timezone issues

Title: Verify responsive design on mobile
Preconditions: 1. Browser set to mobile viewport (375px width) | 2. User navigates to /users
Steps: 1. Check page layout | 2. Test form usability | 3. Test table scrolling | 4. Check navigation | 5. Test all interactions
Expected Result: 1. Navigation collapses appropriately | 2. Form fields stack vertically | 3. Table scrolls horizontally | 4. Touch targets are adequate (min 44px) | 5. Text remains readable | 6. Buttons are accessible | 7. No horizontal page scroll except table

Title: Verify success alert auto-dismiss or persistence
Steps: 1. Submit valid user | 2. Observe success alert | 3. Check if it auto-dismisses or requires manual close | 4. Test dismissal if manual
Expected Result: 1. Success alert appears after submission | 2. Alert behavior is consistent | 3. Alert does not block subsequent form submissions | 4. Alert clears when starting new submission

Title: Test form with name containing only spaces
Steps: 1. Enter "   " (only spaces) in Name | 2. Fill other fields | 3. Submit
Expected Result: 1. Validation treats as empty: "Name is required" | 2. Form blocked | 3. Trim logic applied

Title: Verify pagination or limit on users list
Preconditions: 1. Database has 100+ users | 2. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Check if all users load | 2. Look for pagination controls | 3. Test scrolling behavior | 4. Check performance
Expected Result: 1. If paginated: pagination controls visible and functional | 2. If not: all users load efficiently | 3. No performance degradation | 4. Limit parameter in API (50 users per request per code) | 5. "Load more" or infinite scroll if implemented

Title: Verify database persistence across server restarts
Preconditions: 1. Create user via /users page | 2. Stop backend server | 3. Restart server | 4. Reload /users page
Steps: 1. Create user "Persistence Test" | 2. Note user appears in table | 3. Restart server | 4. Reload page | 5. Check if user still appears
Expected Result: 1. User persists after server restart | 2. Data remains in database | 3. User appears in list after reload | 4. All data fields intact
